chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the Rheonics SRD-X4 flush sensor for hygienic and sanitary applications, detailing its features, benefits, and available process connection norms.
product_names: SRD Inline density and viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

SRD-X4: Flush sensor for Hygienic and Sanitary applications

Modified on: Mon, 18 Nov, 2024 at 10:00 PM

What Products are Involved?
SRD Inline density and viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)

What is the purpose of this article?
To give an overview of the flush version of the SRD sensor (SRD-X4) uses and installation options for hygienic and sanitary applications.

1. Overview
SRD is Rheonics sensor for inline density and viscosity measurement. Read more on SRD here (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).
The flush version of the SRD inline density and viscosity meter, shown in Figure 1 and 2 with a Tri-Clamp connection, is used to mount the density meter in a flush installation. His is a dead-zone free mount in the process. The main advantages of this flush sensor probe are its hygienic design and certification that, along with a dead-zone free installation, means that it is easier to clean and disinfect with standard procedures (e.g. Clean in Place - CIP). This design is also useful for small pipes and process connections smaller (e.g. Tri-Clamp DN 20) than those available with the standard short or long SRD. Review these other variants here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/8100029769).
Following the specified hygienic considerations, a flush installation can considerably reduce or eliminate the possibility of plugging, deposits, and dead zones that can cause systematic errors in the density and viscosity readings of the SRD.

Rheonics SRD flush probe is available with different process connections, some suitable norms are:
*   DIN 11853-1/2/3 min DN20
*   DIN11864-1/2/3 min DN20
*   DIN 11851 min DN20
*   ISO 2852, DIN 32676, BS 4825 Part 3 in combination with Tri-Clamp seals
*   ISO 2853, BS 4825 Part 4 in combination with T-seals
*   Varinline® type T and P with EPDM O-Ring in size B,F,N,G

All process connections mentioned above can be ordered with a hygienic certification, EHEDG and/or 3-A. Contact Rheonics Support Team for more information.

Figure 1: SRD-X4-15T Flush Density Meter and Viscometer

specifications.Process Connection Type Varinline®: Type T and P with EPDM O-Ring (sizes B,F,N,G)

key_facts: The SRD-X4 is a flush sensor designed for hygienic and sanitary applications.
It offers a dead-zone free mount in the process, which is easier to clean and disinfect.
The main advantages of the flush sensor probe include its hygienic design and certification.
The sensor's design is beneficial for small pipes and process connections, such as Tri-Clamp DN 20.
A flush installation can significantly reduce or eliminate plugging, deposits, and dead zones.
Reducing plugging, deposits, and dead zones helps prevent systematic errors in density and viscosity readings.
All process connections can be ordered with hygienic certification, including EHEDG and/or 3-A.
diagram_labels: Figure 1: SRD-X4-15T Flush Density Meter and Viscometer
warnings_notes: All process connections can be ordered with a hygienic certification, EHEDG and/or 3-A.

3. Installation considerations
Key recommendations and concepts about the installation of the SRD sensor are detailed in other articles (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297659) and these apply to the flush sensors. It is important to verify that the sensing area, shown in Figure 3, is always immersed in fluid and any stagnant zones should be avoided. These are defined as any space, inside the sensing area, where fluid transport is insufficient leading to immobile fluid, as well as possible accumulation of solids and deposits, or unwanted material like air.
The installation main considerations are labeled in the next drawing. Flush sensors have the sensing element right after the connection, so dead zones must be avoided at all cost (i.e. long standpipes are clearly ruled out).
key_facts: The Rheonics density and viscosity sensor, type SRD, is EHEDG EL Class I certified for hygienic equipment design.
The EHEDG certificate for SRD is valid until December 31, 2024.

Rheonics GmbH is authorized to apply the 3-A Symbol to its Series SR-HS sensors, SRD-CTN, SR-HS/MR, and HAW-OR-EPD models, conforming to 3-A Sanitary Standards 74-07 for Sensors and Sensor Fittings and Connections.
The 3-A certification for SRD is valid through December 31, 2029.
For proper installation, the sensing area of the SRD sensor must always be completely immersed in fluid.
Stagnant zones within the sensing area, where fluid transport is insufficient, must be avoided to prevent immobile fluid, accumulation of solids, deposits, or air.
Flush sensors require the sensing element to be located immediately after the connection, and long standpipes are explicitly prohibited to prevent dead zones.
warnings_notes: The current 3-A Sanitary Standards, Inc., does not warrant that the holder of an authorization to use the 3-A Symbol will produce products conforming to the 3-A Sanitary Standards or that the products so produced will be properly installed.
It is important to verify that the sensing area, shown in Figure 3, is always immersed in fluid and any stagnant zones should be avoided.

Stagnant zones are defined as any space, inside the sensing area, where fluid transport is insufficient leading to immobile fluid, as well as possible accumulation of solids and deposits, or unwanted material like air.
Dead zones must be avoided at all cost (i.e. long standpipes are clearly ruled out).

chunks.page_summary: This page outlines the installation principles for Rheonics SRD-Flush sensors, including dimensional requirements and best practices, and details various HAW weldolet accessories for achieving flush and hygienic installations of SRD-X1-12G and SRV sensors in different wall thicknesses and with various sealing types.
chunks.extracted_text:
SRD-Flush
Rheonics
Units: mm [inch]
Clearance from SRD tip to any obstruction: 12mm
1. Sensing area (in rectangle) always in fluid
2. No stagnation zones in sensing area
3. Verify clearance at the sensor's tip
Figure 3: SRD-Flush installation principles

4. Installation examples
Some suitable adapters for the SRD flush sensor are mentioned in section 1. Installation will depend on the type of adapter used. Rheonics offers a collection of accessories for installation with flush sensors. These accessories and some other well-known installation cases are shown below.

4.1 Rheonics adapters

a. HAW Accessories
The HAW line of Rheonics weldolets enable the installation of the SRD-X1-12G in tanks and pipes (also suitable for the SRV). This sensor is not configured as flush (X4) but with the HAW accessories a flush installation can be ensured. These weldolets have two versions, for thin walls (around 1.5mm) and thick walls (max. 10mm), and each one has two further variants for Ø16.1x1.6 mm O-ring seal and metal-metal seal. These accessories are suitable for flush hygienic installations.

*   Fig a. HAW-12G-OTN (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/haw-12g-xtn/): Weldolet with a Ø16.1x1.6 mm O-ring seal for thin walls
*   Fig b. HAW-12G-MTN (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/haw-12g-xtn/): Weldolet with metal-metal seal for thin walls
*   Fig c. HAW-12G-OTK (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/haw-12g-xtk/): Weldolet with a Ø16.1x1.6 mm O-ring seal for thick walls
*   Fig d. HAW-12G-MTK (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/haw-12g-xtk/): Weldolet with metal-metal seal for thick walls

HAW Accessories
Fig a. HAW-12G-OTN
Fig b. HAW-12G-MTN
key_facts: The sensing area of the SRD-Flush sensor must always be in fluid.

Clearance at the SRD-Flush sensor's tip must be verified during installation.
Rheonics HAW weldolets enable the installation of SRD-X1-12G sensors in tanks and pipes.

HAW accessories ensure a flush installation for SRD-X1-12G sensors, which are not configured as flush (X4) by default.

Each weldolet version has two variants: for Ø16.1x1.6 mm O-ring seal and for metal-metal seal.
HAW accessories are suitable for flush hygienic installations.

b. Flush Tri-Clamp adapter
This Tri-Clamp connection is a common solution for wall or bottom tank installations. It uses a flush weld-on adapter that Rheonics can provide.
Flush SR-sensors need special attention at installation to ensure good performance. It's short insertion depth is beneficial for small spaces but a good exposure to fluid is needed.

SRD - Tri-Clamp Flush Hygienic Installation

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates standard flush and inline installation methods for Rheonics SRD and SRV viscometer/density sensors, detailing required components and their suppliers.
chunks.extracted_text:
Standard
welded flush flange

SRD-X4-Tri-Clamp 1.5"

Figure 5. Flush installation example with standard TC flush flange

SRD Flush Hygienic Installation
Part number | Part description
---|---
1 | SRD-X4-TC-1.5"
2 | Welded flush flange
3 | Tri-Clamp seal
4 | Grooved flange x2
5 | Bolt x4
6 | Hygienic bolts x4

Figure 6. Exploded view of assembly example with TC flush flange

c. IFC-V40 - Inline flow cell DN40.
This solution allows a perpendicular installation of the SRD sensor probe in a straight DN40 or 1.5" line. This installation requires a Varinline N machined plug provided by Rheonics (PLG-VNM) as the probe connection and the blind plug, as shown in Figure 7.

The Varinline DN40 is currently the smallest GEA Housing compatible with the Rheonics SRD. If smaller housings are required contact Rheonics Support Team.

SRV - Varinline Hygienic Installation Example
Description | Supplier | Order code
---|---|---
Inline viscometer - SRV | Rheonics | [https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411056-srv-x1-12g-g-1-2-SRD-X4-VNM](https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411056-srv-x1-12g-g-1-2-SRD-X4-VNM)
| | [https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412204-srd-varinline-inline-density-and-viscosity-meter-with-varinline-flange](https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412204-srd-varinline-inline-density-and-viscosity-meter-with-varinline-flange)
Varinline Housing DN40 | [https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp](https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) Rheonics | IFC-V40

key_facts: The page illustrates a flush installation example using a standard TC flush flange for an SRD-X4-Tri-Clamp 1.5" sensor.

Figure 6 provides an exploded view of the assembly with a TC flush flange, listing components such as the SRD-X4-TC-1.5" sensor, welded flush flange, Tri-Clamp seal, grooved flanges, bolts, and hygienic bolts.
The IFC-V40 is an inline flow cell designed for perpendicular installation of an SRD sensor probe in a straight DN40 or 1.5" line.
The IFC-V40 installation requires a Varinline N machined plug (PLG-VNM) supplied by Rheonics for both probe connection and as a blind plug, as depicted in Figure 7 (not shown on this page).
The Varinline DN40 is currently the smallest GEA Housing compatible with Rheonics SRD sensors.
Rheonics supplies the Inline viscometer - SRV (Order code SRD-X4-VNM) and the Machined N plug (PLG-VNM).
The Varinline Housing DN40 is provided by GEA, with Rheonics listing its order code as IFC-V40.
warnings_notes: If smaller housings are required contact Rheonics Support Team.

chunks.page_summary: This page details installation options for Rheonics SRD inline viscometers and density sensors, specifically focusing on flush installation within DN40 and DN50 Varinline housings, and lists associated product codes and suppliers.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 7: Flush installation drawing of SRD in DN40 Varinline Housing

4.2 Other installation options
a. Varinline:
The SRD can be installed in a Varinline housing equal to or bigger than DN50.
The design of the Varinline housing ensures no dead zones around the sensor, leading to good flow surrounding the sensing element.

SRD - Varinline Hygienic Installation
Description | Supplier | Order code
Inline density and viscosity meter - SRD | Rheonics | SRD-X4-Varinline N
Varinline housing DN 50 | GEA
(https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) | TDN50

Figure 8: Flush installation drawing of SRD in DN50 Varinline Housing

Note
The SRD has a flush, short and long insertion variants. All have a compatible hygienic design and are EHEDG certified. In this article, focus is on the flush version even if an example has been given in Figure 4 with the short variant. The long insertion pobe design has a unique article here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411193-srd-x5-long-insertion-probe).
key_facts: The SRD can be installed in a Varinline housing equal to or bigger than DN50.
The Varinline housing design ensures no dead zones around the sensor, promoting good flow around the sensing element.
The Inline density and viscosity meter SRD from Rheonics has an order code of SRD-X4-Varinline N.
Varinline housing DN 50 can be sourced from GEA with an order code of TDN50.
The SRD is available in flush, short, and long insertion variants.
All SRD variants possess a compatible hygienic design and are EHEDG certified.
This document's primary focus is on the flush version of the SRD.
Details for the long insertion probe design are available in a separate article on the Rheonics support website.
warnings_notes: The SRD has flush, short, and long insertion variants. All have a compatible hygienic design and are EHEDG certified.
In this article, focus is on the flush version even if an example has been given in Figure 4 with the short variant.
The long insertion pobe design has a unique article here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411193-srd-x5-long-insertion-probe).
